primary_sources.notes: Revenue ~₹54,000 Cr. Employee costs ~₹2,200 Cr (4.1%), Occupancy costs ~₹540 Cr (1.0%), Other expenses ~₹1,300 Cr (2.4%). India's most profitable organised retailer.
Revenue ~₹14,000 Cr. Employee costs ~6%, Rent/occupancy ~10%, Marketing ~5%.
Revenue ~₹5,000 Cr. Employee costs ~8%, Rent ~10%, Advertising ~4%.

primary_sources.source_name: Retailers Association of India (RAI) India Retail 2024
primary_sources.notes: India organised retail cost benchmarks: employee 4-8%, occupancy 6-12% of revenue. Industry structural data.
Retail sector IT spend 1-2.5% of revenue globally; India discount retail lower.

source_metadata.methodology: P25/P50/P75 derived from FY24-25 annual reports of 3 listed Indian organised retailers (DMart, Trent, Shoppers Stop). RAI industry benchmarks used for validation. DMart represents P25 (world-class low-cost retailer), Shoppers Stop/specialty at P75-P90.

HR.source_note: Employee cost % of revenue. DMart FY25: 4.1% (best-in-class EDLC model), Trent FY25: ~6%, Shoppers Stop FY25: ~8%. RAI: Indian organised retail employee cost 4-10%.

FACILITIES.source_note: Occupancy / rent costs. DMart FY25: ~1.0% (own stores strategy). Trent: ~10% (leased stores). Shoppers Stop: ~10% (high-street leased locations). RAI: leased retailers 8-12%.

MARKETING.source_note: Advertising, promotions, customer loyalty programme costs. DMart: ~0.5% (minimal advertising, EDLC model). Trent (Zudio/Westside): ~5%. Shoppers Stop: ~4%. E-commerce: 8-12%.

IT.source_note: IT systems: POS, ERP, loyalty, e-commerce. Gartner Retail IT 2024: 1-2.5% of revenue for omni-channel retailers. Pure-play discount: 0.5-1%. Omni-channel: 2-3%.

LOGISTICS_INDIA.source_note: Supply chain, warehousing, last-mile. RAI: grocery/value retail 1-3%, fashion 4-7%, e-commerce 6-10%.

OUTSOURCED.source_note: Housekeeping, security, outsourced e-commerce fulfilment. INTERNAL_CALIBRATION.

INSURANCE.source_note: Group health, property, inventory insurance. INTERNAL_CALIBRATION.
